medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Frequent pyogenic infections since birth, including oral ulceration, stomatitis, pharyngitis, cutaneous cellulitis, furunculosis, pneumonia, and septicaemia. Chronic periodontitis. Severity of infections ranged from mild to severe, some requiring hospital admission for intravenous antibacterial therapy and drainage of abscesses.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Diagnosed with Kostmann’s syndrome at 4 months of age. Recurrent bacterial infections. Developed myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) progressing to acute myeloid leukemia.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: CT scan revealed three enhancing nodules in the right occipital lobe with perilesional oedema, in keeping with small abscesses. Abscesses in the chest wall, left gluteal region, right groin, both hamstring muscles, and both vastus lateralis muscles. Patchy consolidation in both lung fields. Moderate hepatosplenomegaly.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Neutrophil count of less than 500×10^3 cells/l (normal: 2–7×10^9/l). Monocytosis and eosinophilia may be evident. Total leukocyte counts are frequently normal because of monocytosis. Lymphocyte production is unaffected. Mild anemia may be present from chronic inflammation.
Arrest of neutrophil precursor maturation at the promyelocyte or myelocyte level. Later revealed features suggestive of myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) progressing to acute myeloid leukemia.
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Progression from myelodysplastic syndrome to acute myeloid leukemia.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Three enhancing nodules in the right occipital lobe with perilesional oedema, in keeping with small abscesses.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: CT scan of the chest wall

radiographic_examinations.findings: Abscess in the right iliopsoas and left obturator internus muscles.
Abscesses in both vastus lateralis muscles.
Patchy consolidation in both lung fields.
Patchy consolidation in both lung fields. Moderate hepatosplenomegaly was present.
